text:
Market today: Tech and chip names are facing headwinds. AI spending concerns are spooking investors, and there's specific weakness in analog semiconductors. Rising oil prices add a layer of macro uncertainty, which can be a drag on overall market sentiment.

Your holdings: SOXL is down a painful -36.74%, and SPAL is absolutely hammered at -55.27%. Both are leveraged and decaying ETFs, making these drawdowns extra brutal on margin. SMH is also down -7.77%, reflecting the chip sector's struggles.

Watch: You need to keep a very close eye on SOXL's stop and seriously consider SPAL's extreme loss. Monitor QQQ and QQQI for further signs of tech weakness.

Bottom line: Tech and chips are under pressure; manage risk on your leveraged positions aggressively.
plays: SOXL: This leveraged ETF is bleeding at -36.74%. Respect your stop at 200 to prevent further significant losses.
SPAL: Down -55.27% and leveraged. Consider cutting losses if it doesn't stabilize quickly; this kind of drop on margin is dangerous.
QQQ/QQQI: With tech sentiment softening, watch QQQ's stop at 670 and QQQI's stop at 52 closely. Trimming might be prudent if they break.
